offense: Murder – Killing someone intentionally
Attempted Murder – Trying to kill someone
Culpable Homicide – Causing death without full intent
Theft – Stealing someone's belongings
Burglary – Breaking into a house to steal
Robbery – Stealing using force or threat
Dacoity – Gang robbery by 5 or more people
Fraud / Cheating – Deceiving someone for money or gain
Cybercrime / Identity Theft – Online fraud or stealing someone's personal info
Domestic Violence / Cruelty – Abuse or harassment within the family
Assault – Physically attacking or threatening someone
Harassment – Stalking or outraging someone's modesty
Defamation – Damaging someone's reputation by false statements
Dowry Death (Suspicious death of a bride due to harassment by in-laws within 7 years of marriage)
Abetment of Suicide (Forcing or provoking a spouse/dependent into ending their own life via harassment)
Domestic Hurt (Physical household violence like slapping, kicking, or hitting)
Grievous Domestic Hurt (Intentionally breaking bones or causing permanent injury to a family member)
Acid Attack (Throwing acidic chemicals to burn or disfigure a partner out of domestic revenge)
Sexual Intercourse During Separation (Forcing non-consensual relations with a wife living under a legal separation order)
Deceitful Promise of Marriage (Tricking someone into a relationship using a false promise of marriage or hidden true identity)
Bigamy (Marrying a second person while your first marriage is still legally valid)
Mock Marriage Fraud (Tricking a partner into believing they are legally married via a fake or deceptive ceremony)
Stridhan Misappropriation (Husband or in-laws illegally locking away or refusing to return a bride's personal marriage gifts)
Dowry Death
description: Causing the death of a woman by burns, bodily injury, or cruelty by husband/relatives within 7 years of marriage over dowry demands.

offense: Abetment of Suicide
description: Instigating, conspiring, or intentionally aiding a person into committing suicide.

offense: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt
description: Intentionally causing severe bodily damage (e.g., bone fractures, permanent loss of sight/hearing, disfigurement).

offense: Grievous Hurt by Acid Attack
description: Permanently or partially disfiguring, burning, or maiming a person using acidic corrosive substances.

offense: Kidnapping / Abduction
description: Forcefully or deceitfully taking a person away from their lawful guardianship or location without consent.

offense: Rape
description: Committing non-consensual sexual acts against a person through force, coercion, or misrepresentation.

offense: Gang Rape
description: Where a sexual assault or rape is committed by one or more persons acting in furtherance of a common intention.

offense: Robbery
description: Committing theft or extortion by intentionally causing (or threatening to cause) instant death, hurt, or wrongful restraint.

offense: Dacoity
description: An aggravated form of robbery committed jointly by a gang of five or more armed individuals.

offense: Mob Lynching
description: A new individual offense created when a group of 5 or more people acts together to commit murder/hurt based on race, caste, sex, or belief.

offense: Organized Crime (Gang/Syndicate)
description: Executing continuous unlawful violent acts (like contract killing, extortion, or land grabbing) as a member of a crime syndicate.

offense: Murder (Intentional killing)
Mob Lynching (Murder by a group of 5+ people based on identity)
Attempt to Murder (Trying to kill someone but failing)
Culpable Homicide (Causing death without full intent/premeditation)
Dowry Death (Suspicious death of a bride due to cruelty/harassment)
Abetment of Suicide (Instigating or assisting someone to end their life)
Grievous Hurt (On-purpose severe injury like bone fractures)
Acid Attack (Throwing chemicals to disfigure or burn someone)
Kidnapping & Abduction (Taking someone away against their will)
Sexual Assault / Rape (Non-consensual sexual acts)
Gang Rape (Sexual assault committed by multiple people)
Organized Crime (Continuous gang violence, extortion, or syndicates)
Theft (Stealing someone's belongings when they aren't looking)
Extortion (Blackmailing or threatening someone to force a payout)
Robbery (Stealing from someone face-to-face using force or threats)
Dacoity (Armed gang robbery by 5 or more individuals)
Criminal Misappropriation (Keeping lost money/property for yourself)
Criminal Breach of Trust (Embezzling assets you were trusted to manage)
Receiving Stolen Property (Buying or holding items you know are stolen)
Cheating (Tricking or scamming someone out of money using deception)
Mischief (Intentionally damaging or breaking someone else's property)
Arson / Mischief by Fire (Destroying property using fire/explosives)
Criminal Trespass & Burglary (Breaking into property without permission)
House-Breaking by Night (Forced entry into a house between sunset and sunrise)
Physical Attack / Shoving
description: Actually hitting, pushing, slapping, or throwing something at someone without their permission.

offense: Making a Move to Hit / Threatening
description: You haven’t touched them yet, but you raise your fist or weapon to make them think you are about to strike.

offense: Locking Someone Up
description: Completely trapping someone within a closed space (like a room or a car) so they cannot escape.

offense: Ghuspaith (Illegal Entry)
description: Walking into or staying on someone's private property without their permission.

offense: Filing a Fake Case
description: Dragging someone to court or the police station with completely fabricated lies just to ruin their life.

offense: Disturbing the Neighborhood
description: Doing things that cause general annoyance or danger to everyone around (like blasting music at 3 AM or dumping trash on the street).

offense: Keeping Someone's Belongings
description: Taking someone's personal items (like a laptop or a bike) and treating it like your own or refusing to return it.

offense: Extreme Carelessness
description: Being so recklessly careless that your actions cause a major accident or kill someone (like extreme drunk driving).

offense: Abusing to Humiliate
description: Using deeply offensive, abusive words face-to-face to break someone's mental peace and provoke a fight.

offense: Scam / Cheating
description: Tricking someone with smooth lies or fake promises to get them to hand over their hard-earned money.

offense: Criminal Breach of Trust (Embezzling or stealing funds you were legally trusted to protect/manage)
Forgery (Creating a fake document, electronic record, or altering signatures to scam someone)
Forgery for Cheating (Making fake legal papers or corporate IDs specifically to execute a financial fraud)
Using a Forged Document as Genuine (Knowingly presenting fake business invoices, bills, or wills as real)
Falsification of Accounts (An employee altering or destroying office bookkeeping/ledgers to hide missing cash)
Counterfeiting Currency or Bank Notes (Printing fake physical money or bonds with the intent to pass them off as real)
Bribery by Public Servants (A government official accepting an illegal payout to manipulate a legal duty)
Money Laundering (Disguising illegally earned money as legitimate, untraceable income through asset tracking)
Corporate Fraud (Deceiving company shareholders or the public to gain unfair business/monetary advantages)
Tax Evasion (Intentionally making false statements or fabricating receipts to avoid paying mandatory taxes)
